docs.title: Spacewalks
docs.text:
Analysis of NASA Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA) data.

This project reads JSON spacewalk records, cleans and processes the data, outputs a CSV summary, and generates a cumulative time plot of hours spent in space over time.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/thatgardnerone/spacewalks.git
cd spacewalks
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
python eva_data_analysis.py

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
• Tutorials — step-by-step guides for getting started
  • How-To Guides — practical recipes for common tasks
  • Reference — API reference for all functions
  • Explanation — background and design decisions

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Extra-Vehicular Activity (EVA), commonly known as a spacewalk, is any activity performed by an astronaut or cosmonaut outside the spacecraft. NASA maintains a public dataset of all EVAs conducted since 1965.

docs.title: Data pipeline
docs.text:
The Spacewalks analysis pipeline performs the following steps:

  1. Read: Load EVA records from a JSON file into a pandas DataFrame.
  2. Clean: Remove rows with missing duration or date values.
  3. Export: Write the cleaned data to CSV for downstream use.
  4. Plot: Calculate cumulative hours spent in space and generate a time-series plot.

docs.title: Design decisions
docs.text:
• Duration format: The raw data stores durations as H:MM strings. The text_to_duration function converts these to decimal hours for numerical analysis.
  • Crew size parsing: Crew members are listed as semicolon-separated strings. The calculate_crew_size function counts the number of semicolons (each crew member's name is followed by a semicolon).
  • Cumulative plot: The cumulative time plot provides a visual summary of how total spacewalk hours have accumulated over the decades, highlighting periods of increased EVA activity.

docs.title: How to change the output file path
docs.text:
By default, the analysis script writes its CSV output to results/eva-data.csv. To change this, pass custom file paths as command-line arguments:

python eva_data_analysis.py data/eva-data.json path/to/custom-output.csv

Both arguments must be provided together — the first is the input JSON file and the second is the output CSV path.

docs.title: How to run tests
docs.text:
Run the full test suite with:

python -m pytest

To include a coverage report:

python -m pytest --cov

To generate an HTML coverage report:

python -m pytest --cov --cov-report=html

The HTML report will be available in the htmlcov/ directory.

docs.title: How to build the documentation locally
docs.text:
Install the documentation dependencies:

pip install mkdocs mkdocstrings[python] mkdocs-material

Build and serve the docs locally:

mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser.

docs.title: add_crew_size_variable(df_)
docs.text:
Add crew size (crew_size) variable to the dataset.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df_ DataFrame

The input data frame.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description df_copy DataFrame

A copy of df_ with the new crew_size variable added.

Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def add_crew_size_variable(df_):
    """Add crew size (crew_size) variable to the dataset.

    Args:
        df_ (pd.DataFrame): The input data frame.

    Returns:
        df_copy (pd.DataFrame): A copy of df_ with the new crew_size variable added.
    """
    print('Adding crew size variable (crew_size) to dataset')
    df_copy = df_.copy()
    df_copy["crew_size"] = df_copy["crew"].apply(
        calculate_crew_size
    )
    return df_copy

docs.title: add_duration_hours(df)

Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def add_duration_hours(df):
    """Add duration in hours as a new column to the dataframe.

    Args:
        df (pd.DataFrame): The input dataframe.

    Returns:
        df_copy (pd.DataFrame): A copy of df with the new duration_hours column added.
    """
    df_copy = df.copy()
    df_copy['duration_hours'] = df_copy['duration'].apply(text_to_duration)
    return df_copy

docs.text:

Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def calculate_crew_size(crew):
    """Calculate crew_size for a single crew entry.

    Args:
        crew (str): The text entry in the crew column.

    Returns:
        int: The crew size.
    """
    if crew.split() == []:
        return None
    else:
        return len(re.split(r';', crew)) - 1

docs.title: main(input_file, output_file, graph_file)
docs.text:
Main function to execute the EVA data analysis pipeline.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default input_file str

The path to the input JSON file.

required output_file str

The path to the output CSV file.

required graph_file str

The path to the output graph file.

required Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def main(input_file, output_file, graph_file):
    """Main function to execute the EVA data analysis pipeline.

    Args:
        input_file (str): The path to the input JSON file.
        output_file (str): The path to the output CSV file.
        graph_file (str): The path to the output graph file.
    """
    print("--START--")

    # Read the EVA dataset from JSON
    print(f'Reading JSON file {input_file}')
    eva_df = read_json_to_dataframe(input_file)

    # Write the cleaned data to CSV for downstream use
    print(f'Saving CSV file to {output_file}')
    write_dataframe_to_csv(eva_df, output_file)

    # Plot cumulative time spent in space over the years
    eva_df.sort_values('date', inplace=True)
    print(f'Plotting cumulative time in space to {graph_file}')
    plot_cumulative_time_in_space(eva_df, graph_file)

    print("--END--")

docs.title: plot_cumulative_time_in_space(df, graph_file)
docs.text:
Plot the cumulative time spent in space over the years.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df DataFrame

The input dataframe.

required graph_file str

The path to the output graph file.

required Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def plot_cumulative_time_in_space(df, graph_file):
    """Plot the cumulative time spent in space over the years.

    Args:
        df (pd.DataFrame): The input dataframe.
        graph_file (str): The path to the output graph file.
    """
    df = add_duration_hours(df)
    df['cumulative_time'] = df['duration_hours'].cumsum()

    plt.plot(df['date'], df['cumulative_time'], 'ko-')
    plt.xlabel('Year')
    plt.ylabel('Total time spent in space to date (hours)')
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(graph_file)
    plt.show()

docs.title: read_json_to_dataframe(input_file)
docs.text:
Read the data from a JSON file into a Pandas dataframe.

Cleans the data by removing any incomplete rows and sort by date.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default input_file str

The path to the JSON file.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description eva_df DataFrame

The cleaned and sorted data as a dataframe structure.

Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def read_json_to_dataframe(input_file):
    """Read the data from a JSON file into a Pandas dataframe.

    Cleans the data by removing any incomplete rows and sort by date.

    Args:
        input_file (str): The path to the JSON file.

    Returns:
        eva_df (pd.DataFrame): The cleaned and sorted data as a dataframe structure.
    """
    eva_df = pd.read_json(input_file, convert_dates=['date'], encoding='ascii')
    eva_df['eva'] = eva_df['eva'].astype(float)
    eva_df.dropna(axis=0, subset=['duration', 'date'], inplace=True)
    return eva_df

docs.title: summarise_categorical(df_, varname_)
docs.text:
Tabulate the distribution of a categorical variable.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df_ DataFrame

The input dataframe.

required varname_ str

The name of the variable.

required

Returns:

Type Description

pd.DataFrame: dataframe containing the count and percentage of

each unique value of varname_.

Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def summarise_categorical(df_, varname_):
    """Tabulate the distribution of a categorical variable.

    Args:
        df_ (pd.DataFrame): The input dataframe.
        varname_ (str): The name of the variable.

    Returns:
        pd.DataFrame: dataframe containing the count and percentage of
        each unique value of varname_.
    """
    print(f'Tabulating distribution of categorical variable {varname_}')

    # Prepare statistical summary
    count_variable = df_[[varname_]].copy()
    count_summary = count_variable.value_counts(dropna=False)
    percentage_summary = round(count_summary / count_variable.size, 2) * 100

    # Combine results into a summary data frame
    df_summary = pd.concat([count_summary, percentage_summary], axis=1)
    df_summary.columns = ['count', 'percentage']
    df_summary.sort_index(inplace=True)

    df_summary = df_summary.reset_index()
    return df_summary

docs.title: text_to_duration(duration)
docs.text:
Convert a text format duration "HH:MM" to duration in hours.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default duration str

The text format duration.

required

Returns:

Name Type Description duration_hours float

The duration in hours.

Source code in eva_data_analysis.py 
def text_to_duration(duration):
    """Convert a text format duration "HH:MM" to duration in hours.

    Args:
        duration (str): The text format duration.

    Returns:
        duration_hours (float): The duration in hours.
    """
    hours, minutes = duration.split(":")
    duration_hours = int(hours) + int(minutes) / 60
    return duration_hours

def write_dataframe_to_csv(df, output_file):
    """Write the dataframe to a CSV file.

    Args:
        df (pd.DataFrame): The input dataframe.
        output_file (str): The path to the output CSV file.
    """
    df.to_csv(output_file, index=False, encoding='utf-8')

docs.title: Analysing spacewalk data with custom output paths
docs.text: This tutorial walks you through running the Spacewalks analysis pipeline with custom input and output file paths.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Make sure you have completed the installation steps.

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare your environment
docs.text:
Activate the virtual environment:

source .venv/bin/activate

docs.title: Step 2: Run with default settings
docs.text:
Run the analysis with default input and output paths:

python eva_data_analysis.py

This reads from data/eva-data.json and writes output to results/eva-data.csv and results/cumulative_eva_graph.png.

docs.title: Step 3: Specify custom file paths
docs.text:
You can provide custom input and output file paths as command-line arguments:

python eva_data_analysis.py data/eva-data.json my-output.csv

The first argument is the path to the input JSON file, and the second is the path to the output CSV file. The graph is always saved to results/cumulative_eva_graph.png.

docs.title: Step 4: Check the output
docs.text:
Verify that the output CSV file was created and contains the expected data:

head my-output.csv

You should see a CSV file with columns including eva, country, crew, vehicle, date, duration, and purpose.
